schema.description: Cross-language keystone for the Collision Engineers EVA drag-drop JSON payload. The TypeScript serializer (mockup-app/src/contracts/eva-export.ts) and the Python parser Function (cedocumentmapper_v2.0) both target this schema. Exactly the 12 settled snake_case fields in contract order; vrm and reference are Case-identity fields and are intentionally NOT part of this payload. (Engineer allocation is NOT an EVA submission field — it is left blank and assigned inside EVA after submission; removed from the contract per the product owner's ruling, B3 RESOLVED.)

inspection_address.description: Field 9. Exactly six newline-separated lines, OR the literal 'Image Based Assessment'.

schema.title: EVA payload (12-field, settled contract)

schema.description: VENDORED COPY (ships inside the parser deployment package). Source of truth is the repo-root contracts/eva-payload.schema.json; this copy exists because .funcignore can only ship files INSIDE functions/parser/, and the FC1 deployment package therefore cannot reach the repo-root contracts/ dir. schema_validation._default_schema_path() resolves this package-local copy when deployed, the repo-root copy in local dev/tests. Kept identical by tests/test_schema_vendored_in_sync.py. Cross-language keystone for the Collision Engineers EVA drag-drop JSON payload. Exactly the 12 settled snake_case fields in contract order; vrm and reference are Case-identity fields and are intentionally NOT part of this payload.

inspection_address.description: Field 9. Exactly six newline-separated lines, OR the literal 'Image Based Assessment'.

schema.title: EVA payload (12-field, settled contract)

audit_phrases.description: Signal an AUDIT re-inspection instruction (rules/engine.py detect_audit_signals).

billing_keywords.description: Signal an invoice/fee request for work already carried out.

cancellation_phrases.description: Signal the sender is cancelling/withdrawing an existing instruction, claim or inspection.

chase_phrases.description: High-precision chase wording -- asking us to send a report we already owe.

diminution_phrases.description: Signal a Diminution in Value engagement (distinct case-type, 'D.' Case/PO marker; rules/engine.py detect_case_type_signals). Review-first downstream until grounded on a real inbound instruction.

dual_report_audit_phrases.description: Signal a DUAL 'report + audit report' instruction -- one letter commissioning both a standard report AND an audit (the QDOS template; rules/engine.py detect_case_type_signals). The dual form changes downstream Case/PO numbering (collisionspike ADR-0021).

image_service_delivery_phrases.description: The completed-capture delivery shape ('completed lead', 'damage capture') -- the signal that the service is DELIVERING captured vehicle images/quote (collisionspike TKT-102). Requires an image_service identity anchor (domain or phrase); deliberately never keyed on subject emoji.

image_service_identity_phrases.description: Body/footer wording that identifies an image-capture delivery service when the sender domain is unavailable or the mail is forwarded ('powered by tractable'). An identity anchor alone does not fire Rule 0f -- a delivery phrase must corroborate.

image_service_sender_domains.description: Sender DOMAINS of image-capture delivery services CE itself commissions (collisionspike TKT-102: Tractable). An email from one of these domains is a candidate image delivery, never a work provider instructing new work. Matched exactly or as a subdomain suffix (email_classifier.py Rule 0f).

informal_work_keywords.description: Weaker, informal work-request wording; only promotes when corroborated elsewhere.

payment_phrases.description: Signal an INBOUND payment notification -- a remittance advice or payment-transfer notice for work we did (collisionspike TKT-105/TKT-120). The mirror-image of billing_keywords (which is a REQUEST for our invoice): these say money has been / is being sent, so the email routes to billing/payment_remittance, never receiving_work.

pre_instruction_phrases.description: Signal PRE-INSTRUCTION directions -- the sender is telling us what to do WHEN the official instruction later arrives (collisionspike TKT-084). Anchored to a future-instruction reference ('when you receive an instruction'), never bare direction verbs, so a live instruction email cannot trip it.

query_keywords.description: Signal a question/chase/enquiry rather than an instruction.

report_structure_phrases.description: Weaker report body/findings wording; only promotes when corroborated by an engineer_report provider match.

report_title_phrases.description: A genuine engineer's report's own front-matter/heading wording (detection/attachment_typing.py).

summary_markers.description: Case-summary / digest recap markers (not a fresh instruction).

vrm_stopword_trigrams.description: Common English words a well-formed VRM's 3-letter alpha group can accidentally spell (email_classifier.py); a genuine set, order-independent -- loaded as a frozenset.

work_keywords.description: Signal an email is instructing new work (formal instruction wording).

schema.description: The externalized flat keyword/phrase string collections consumed by the email-triage classifier (rules/email_classifier.py, via rules/engine.py) and content-based attachment typing (detection/attachment_typing.py). Scope is deliberately narrow (collisionspike rules-engine-v2 plan, Phase 5): regexes, rule ordering, confidence bands and suppression logic all stay in Python -- this schema covers ONLY the flat phrase/keyword lists. additionalProperties is false throughout so a typo'd key (or a stray extra one) fails validation loudly instead of silently missing a rule.
schema.title: Triage Rule Phrase Data
